judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements: a hand-drawn map of Japan with six observing stations, a path line, a red X near Gifu, and a bottom-left annotation; the main difference is that Description B adds a legend in the bottom right corner that Description A omits, but they do not contradict each other.
original_image_tags: A hand-drawn map of Japan showing several locations marked as observing stations (●). A line (—) labeled "PROBABLE PATH OF PHENOMENON" connects several of these stations. A red cross (✕) labeled "PROBABLE AREA OF EXPLOSION OR DISAPPEARANCE" is marked near one station. The observing stations are labeled: FUKUSHIMA, NAGANO, TAKAYAMA, SUWA, IIDA, and GIFU. The map includes a grid overlay. In the bottom left corner, there is a handwritten annotation: "Incl 4³".
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Hand-drawn map of central and northern Japan with coastline outlines, latitude-longitude grid lines, labeled observing stations (Fukushima, Nagano, Takayama, Suwa, Gifu, Iida), a diagonal probable path line, an X marking a probable area of explosion or disappearance, and a legend in the lower right.*
